title: Does culture make emotion?
description:
Franz Boas helps us solve the puzzle of where our emotional lives originate: in our selves or in the cultures around us

- by Noga Arikha
hook: Why do some cultures cry at weddings and others at funerals? A philosophical investigation into emotion as invention, not universal truth.

title: You Can’t Uncast a Spell
description:
One could argue that Gregory Maguire’s novel, Wicked, and its Broadway adaptation are entirely different stories. Alongside the stage musical’s revision of key character personalities, relationships, and even fates, it also softens the novel’s highly adult themes for a more diverse audience. But what binds the two together is an understanding that Wicked is more […]

The post You Can’t Uncast a Spell appeared fi
hook: Wicked the musical rewrote the novel so completely it became a different story—what gets lost when adaptation means softening the adult rage?

title: 'My family is enough': Jamilah Lemieux on being a 'Black. Single. Mother.'
description: As a culture critic, Lemieux has spent years pushing back against the stereotypes and stigma that follow single mothers. Her new book blends her own memoir with the stories of 21 other Black women.
hook: A culture critic spent years dismantling the myth of the 'struggling single mother.' Her new book lets 22 Black women speak for themselves.

title: Tanya Tagaq – Saputjiji
description:
Tanya Tagaq

Saputjiji

The award-winning Inuk throat singer and composer returns with her seventh album, as vital and terrifying as ever

Saputjiji by Tanya Tagaq

Twelve years have passed since Tanya Tagaq took a sledgehammer to the polite veneer of the Canadian music industry, winning the Polaris Music Prize and a Juno for Animis
hook: Tanya Tagaq's seventh album channels her Inuk throat-singing practice into something vital, terrifying, and entirely her own—music as spiritual currency.

title: Please, No More Disaffected White Girls
description: Anika Jade Levy’s “Flat Earth” is navel-gazing, ouroboric, masturbatory — a Dimes Square novel for Dimes Square people.
hook: A brutal takedown of the literary trend: privileged white women writing about their own emptiness as if it's profound. When does navel-gazing become parody?
